evidence.scientific_clarity: Table clearly shows 4 methods x 5 benchmarks; OursModel leads all columns; bold means (CMBX10 font confirmed) correctly highlight best/overlapping entries per brief spec.
evidence.typography: pdfTeX-generated with fully embedded Type1 CM fonts (CMBX10, CMR10, CMTI10, CMMI10, CMSY10); no DejaVu Sans; selectable text; appropriate 10pt size for camera-ready.
evidence.palette: Blue row highlight (RGB 240,248,255) correctly distinguishes 'Ours' group; no colorblind-problematic red/green directionality; but highlight is cosmetic, not data-encoding.
evidence.layout_density: Page sized 7x3 inches to fit table snugly; booktabs rules with no vertical lines; no gaping whitespace; group labels and data rows well-spaced.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption explains bold convention and seed count correctly; table.tex required by brief is missing from output directory, which a reviewer would flag; std not in subscript style as specified.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF produced by pdfTeX 1.40.29 with fully embedded, subsetted Type1 fonts; all text selectable (confirmed via pdftotext); inherently vector — no raster elements.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (59KB) and figure.png (2100x900 @ 300dpi) present; caption.tex present; but table.tex is absent from work directory despite being an explicit required output in the brief.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states bold convention, seed count (5), and group structure; mentions blue highlight; but lacks a specific numerical finding or claim (e.g., 'OursModel achieves 76.8% on MMLU, +3.3pp over the best baseline').

overall_strengths: Fully vector PDF with proper embedded CM fonts from pdfTeX — text is selectable and publication-ready without any matplotlib font hacks needed.
Bold winner detection logic is correct and confirmed in the rendered PDF (CMBX10 used for best-mean values); blue row highlight for 'Ours' group renders correctly.
overall_weaknesses: table.tex is missing from the output directory — the brief explicitly requires it as a standalone compilable LaTeX source, making the submission incomplete.

std values use inline {\pm x.xx} style rather than the brief-specified subscript style $72.4_{\pm 1.2}$, and \textbf wraps only the mean digits leaving the ± part in regular weight — inconsistent cell formatting.
